title: Is it possible to select which calendar the Google Calendar Integration will create an event in?
excerpt: Hi all, Is it possible to select which calendar the Google Calendar Integration will create an event in?  I don’t remember seeing any options to select a calendar when setting up the Integration so assume it selects the …

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all, Is it possible to select which calendar the Google Calendar Integration will create an event in? I don’t remember seeing any options to select a calendar when setting up the Integration so assume it selects the default calendar in Google Calendar. But I hope I’m wrong…
cheers, Patrick

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Patrick,

I am afraid at this stage it isn’t possible to customise the calendar for the event to be created in… the event will be created in the default calendar in Google… I apologise for the setback here Patrick

posts.author: botsquad (Patrick Fallon)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the answer, it’s what I expected. I have to say, the Google Calendar Integration (and the Monday Calendar view) is one my greatest headaches with Monday…

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: We hear you and do apologise to hear of your frustration. This is important feedback that I’ll pass onto the relevant teams.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @botsquad

We developed an app called Google Calendar Integration Plus that provides a robust Google calendar and monday.com integration.
Here’s how it can be used in this case →

Simply select the calendar you want to connect →

Screenshot 2024-07-13 at 20.55.181966×402 21.7 KB

Here are the key differences from monday’s native solution -

  1. Allows to select which calendar to use (not just the primary default)
  2. Supports subitems
  3. Much more flexible triggers - “no more sync every item”
  4. Allows full mapping - updates, invitees and more
  5. Allows to control the start & end date and time.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - Google Calendar Integration Plus - monday.com App Marketplace

Hope that helps

posts.author: YashGarg (Yash Garg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @botsquad ,

We recently launched the app Google Calendar Sync - monday.com App Marketplace, which allows you to choose any of your calendars instead of just being restricted to your primary calendar. It will fit your use case perfectly.

Other Features

  1. Ability to select any calendar instead of default one
  2. Ability to create event on status column change
  3. Ability to configure realtime 1-way sync and 2-way sync as per need
  4. Ability to send invites to team members and guests from monday itself

If you want to see it in action checkout our demo video here
